stories.headline: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: US-Iran peace deal talks underway in Switzerland
stories.summary: "We're going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue," US Vice President JD Vance told reporters.

stories.headline: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
stories.summary: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.

stories.headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubbl

stories.headline: Bolivian authorities say no active blockades after state of emergency decree
stories.summary: State of emergency permits deployment of military to support police amid five weeks of anti-government protests.

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes hit oil facilities in Crimea, Russia’s Krasnodar
stories.summary: Crimea's Moscow-installed leader says four people killed in drone attack on the Russian-occupied Black Sea peninsula.

stories.headline: US and Iran begin talks on initial peace deal in Switzerland
stories.summary: The face-to-face meeting follows ongoing fighting in Lebanon and Iran's claim to have shut the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.headline: US-Iran delegations arrive as talks begin in Switzerland
stories.summary: US-Iran delegations arrive as talks begin in Switzerland.

stories.headline: Senator Warnock Calls Republican Party a Cult
stories.summary: Senator Raphael Warnock (D-Georgia) delivers a pointed critique of the current state of the Republican Party, describing it as a cult centered around loyalty to President Donald Trump rather than principles or ideas. Watch the Senator discuss with David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend how progress is not automatic, Americans need to resist despair and to continue advocating for positive change. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Stockpiles Near Danger Zone
stories.summary: Energy markets expert and author of Oil's Endless Bid Dan Dicker says oil markets are underestimating the impact of ongoing supply disruptions, warning that global stockpiles have been drawn down significantly as millions of barrels per day remain unable to reach end markets. Dicker warns hosts of Bloomberg This Weekend David Gura and Christina Ruffini if inventories continue to fall, crude prices could surge from current levels to as high as $13

stories.headline: How Trump’s Fragile Agreement With Iran Is Shaping the Midterms
stories.summary: Democrats say the president started an economically painful war that resulted in nothing positive. Republicans are more divided, even as they show some signs of relief at falling gas prices.

stories.headline: Malaysia's Anwar: All's well that ends well?
Starmer reflecting on 'political realities' amid reports of imminent resignation
stories.summary: The British PM has repeatedly vowed to fight on in his role but has come under increasing pressure in recent weeks.

stories.headline: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
stories.summary: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

stories.headline: Why Giorgia Meloni turned on Donald Trump

stories.headline: A $2.8 Trillion Asset Management Star Is a Deal Away From Losing Its Crown
stories.summary: Amundi became Europe’s largest fund firm with the help of acquisitions. But after years without a major takeover, rivals are catching up.

stories.headline: UK's Starmer Faces Growing Pressure to Step Down
stories.summary: With escalating political turmoil surrounding the the UK's Prime Minister Keir Starmer, the PM has reportedly contacted cabinet ministers amid mounting speculation about his potential departure. Anchor of Bloomberg's Daybreak Europe Lizzy Burden explains to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend how the situation appears to be reaching a tipping point. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Guinea’s President Doumbouya Announces Ban on Raw Gold Exports
stories.summary: Guinean President Mamadi Doumbouya announced a ban on raw gold exports, in an effort to boost local processing of the metal and help the domestic economy.

stories.headline: Soaring Profits in EM Build the Case for a Raging Bull Market
stories.summary: For the first time in four years, companies in emerging markets are beating profit estimates, giving investors a fresh reason to believe the bull market is just getting started.

stories.headline: Australia is publishing books too quickly – and everyone is losing out
stories.summary: Thanks to rushed deadlines, financial pressure and overworked staff, titles are going to market before they’re ready – and then sliding from view immediatelyA Sydney author – I’ll call her Rebecca – vowed never to write another book after the deranging experience of publishing her first. stories.headline: Iran Sanctions Losing Efficacy
stories.summary: In 2015, sanctions played a key role in bringing Iran to the negotiating table but now warfare has been the primary tool. Oliver Wyman Partner and Global Anti-Financial Crime Practice Leader Daniel Tannebaum explains to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend how even if sanctions on Iran are lifted, businesses may remain hesitant to reengage with Iran due to lingering risks. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: What We Know About the Los Angeles Warehouse Fire

stories.headline: 'It was surreal': British couple describe having warning shots fired near them by Russian warship

stories.summary: Japan needs to revisit the pacifist posture that has defined it since World War Two, Shinjiro Koizumi says.

stories.headline: Dáil passes abortion bill to remove three-day wait

stories.headline: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages

stories.headline: Cannabis commercialisation not decriminalisation drives up usage, study finds

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.
